breaches.title: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
breaches.tldr: The GoldenEyeDog subgroup is implicated in a data breach at DigiCert, leading to the theft of code-signing certificates. This compromise allows attackers to sign malicious software with legitimate certificates, evading security controls and trust mechanisms.
breaches.action: Revoke any code-signing certificates associated with DigiCert that may have been compromised. Implement strict software integrity checks and monitor for executables signed with suspicious or newly revoked certificates. Update trust policies to reflect the breach.

breaches.title: Ernst & Young discloses data breach after support system hack
breaches.tldr: Ernst & Young has confirmed a data breach resulting from the compromise of a third-party support ticket system used by its IT staff. Customer data may have been exposed, leading to potential privacy violations and further targeted attacks.
breaches.action: Notify potentially affected customers immediately. Investigate the full scope of the breach, including what specific data was accessed or exfiltrated. Implement enhanced security measures for all third-party vendors and conduct a thorough review of incident response plans.

ransomware.title: Coca-Cola says Fairlife ransomware attack halts US dairy production
ransomware.tldr: The Coca-Cola Company disclosed today that a ransomware attack impacting its Fairlife dairy subsidiary has disrupted operations, temporarily suspending production of Fairlife products across the United States. This highlights the significant operational and supply chain impacts of ransomware.
ransomware.action: Isolate affected systems, restore from secure backups, and conduct a thorough forensic investigation to identify the attack vector and extent of compromise. Enhance network segmentation, implement robust endpoint protection, and enforce multi-factor authentication across the enterprise.

ransomware.action: Implement robust incident response procedures, including isolating affected systems, engaging forensic experts, and restoring operations from secure backups. Review and enhance cybersecurity defenses, focusing on ransomware prevention, endpoint detection, and network segmentation.

vulns.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
vulns.tldr: CISA has added CVE-2026-58644, a Remote Code Execution (RCE) zero-day vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. This critical flaw is actively being exploited in the wild, posing an immediate and severe risk to SharePoint deployments.
vulns.action: Immediately apply the latest security patches for Microsoft SharePoint that address CVE-2026-58644. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement temporary mitigations such as network segmentation and restricting access to SharePoint servers, while preparing for urgent patching.
vulns.affected_versions: Microsoft SharePoint. Refer to official Microsoft advisories for CVE-2026-58644 for specific vulnerable versions.
vulns.how_to_determine_impact: Check all deployed Microsoft SharePoint servers for the presence of the vulnerability by verifying patch levels against the official Microsoft security advisory for CVE-2026-58644. Look for signs of compromise on unpatched systems, such as unusual file creations, process executions, or network connections from SharePoint servers.
vulns.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications with Name contains 'SharePoint' and Version not in '[patched_versions_list]'. Additionally, search for suspicious processes or network connections from SharePoint servers.
vulns.rapid7_query_hint: Use InsightVM to scan assets for 'vulnerability.cve_id = 'CVE-2026-58644'' or 'vulnerability.title CONTAINS 'SharePoint RCE''. Monitor SharePoint server logs with InsightIDR for anomalous activity.

vulns.title: New Windows LegacyHive zero-day gives hackers admin privileges
vulns.tldr: A security researcher has released a Windows zero-day exploit, dubbed LegacyHive, that allows attackers to escalate privileges on up-to-date Windows systems. This flaw grants administrative access, posing a significant risk to the integrity and security of Windows environments.
vulns.action: Monitor for official patches from Microsoft addressing the LegacyHive zero-day. In the interim, implement enhanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) rules to detect and block exploitation attempts. Restrict administrative privileges for users and implement application whitelisting.
vulns.affected_versions: Up-to-date Windows systems. Specific vulnerable versions would need to be confirmed by Microsoft's advisory.
vulns.how_to_determine_impact: Perform internal vulnerability scans on all Windows endpoints. Monitor security logs for indications of privilege escalation attempts or unusual process executions consistent with LegacyHive. Await official Microsoft guidance for precise detection methods.
vulns.tanium_query_hint: Get OS Platform and OS Version from all machines. Search for 'Get Running Processes with parent process name not in ('System', 'services.exe') and Effective User Name equals 'SYSTEM'' to look for suspicious privilege escalation.
vulns.rapid7_query_hint: Search InsightVM for 'asset.os.family = 'Windows' AND asset.vulnerability.description CONTAINS 'LegacyHive''. Use InsightIDR to monitor for suspicious processes with SYSTEM privileges being spawned from non-system processes.

vulns.title: CISA urges immediate action on actively exploited Fortinet flaws
vulns.tldr: CISA has issued an urgent directive for federal agencies to patch two actively exploited vulnerabilities in the Fortinet FortiSandbox threat detection platform. These flaws pose a critical risk, enabling attackers to compromise systems if not remediated immediately.
vulns.action: Immediately apply all available security updates and patches for Fortinet FortiSandbox appliances. Review FortiSandbox logs for any signs of compromise or unusual activity, and restrict network access to the management interface.
vulns.affected_versions: Fortinet FortiSandbox threat detection platform. Specific vulnerable versions should be referenced from official Fortinet advisories.
vulns.how_to_determine_impact: Identify all deployed Fortinet FortiSandbox devices. Check the installed firmware versions against Fortinet's security advisories for the actively exploited vulnerabilities. Monitor device logs for unauthorized access or suspicious configurations.
vulns.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications with Name contains 'FortiSandbox' and Version not in '[patched_versions_list]'. Additionally, use 'Get Network Connections from all FortiSandbox appliances to external IPs not in whitelist'.
vulns.rapid7_query_hint: Search InsightVM for 'asset.software.name = 'FortiSandbox' AND vulnerability.exploit_available = true'. Monitor FortiSandbox logs and network traffic with InsightIDR for exploitation attempts.

vulns.title: Fresh SharePoint Vulnerability Exploited Soon After Disclosure
vulns.tldr: A newly disclosed vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint is already being actively exploited in the wild, underscoring the urgency for organizations to apply patches. vulns.action: Immediately apply the latest security patches for Microsoft SharePoint. If patching is not feasible, implement temporary mitigations such as network segmentation and restricting external access to SharePoint servers. Conduct a forensic review for signs of compromise on affected systems.
vulns.affected_versions: Microsoft SharePoint. Specific vulnerable versions should be referenced from official Microsoft advisories.
vulns.how_to_determine_impact: Check all deployed Microsoft SharePoint servers for the presence of the vulnerability. Verify patch levels against the official Microsoft security advisory. vulns.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications with Name contains 'SharePoint' and Version not in '[patched_versions_list]'. Use 'Get Network Connections from all SharePoint servers where destination IP is not in internal ranges'.

2026-06-23.title: New macOS ClickFix attack silently mounts DMGs to push infostealer

2026-06-23.action: Implement robust Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) solutions on all macOS devices to detect and block unusual Terminal command executions or silent DMG mounting activities. 2026-06-23.how_to_determine_impact: Monitor macOS endpoint logs for unexpected `hdiutil` (disk image utility) commands, particularly those executed silently or initiated by suspicious processes. Check for the presence of known infostealer malware families or unusual files in user download directories or temporary folders.
2026-06-23.tanium_query_hint: Use 'Get Running Processes[Name, CommandLine]' from all macOS machines to identify suspicious `hdiutil` commands or other unexpected process activity. Additionally, use 'Get File Exists[path]' for known infostealer file paths or indicators of compromise.
2026-06-23.rapid7_query_hint: Utilize InsightAgent queries on macOS assets to detect unusual process execution patterns or file modifications related to DMG mounting and infostealer installation. Look for `process.command_line CONTAINS 'hdiutil attach'` without corresponding user interaction logs.

2026-07-17.title: CISA urges immediate action on actively exploited Fortinet flaws
2026-07-17.tldr: CISA has issued an alert urging federal agencies to immediately patch two actively exploited vulnerabilities in Fortinet's FortiSandbox threat detection platform. These flaws pose a critical risk, indicating real-world exploitation in the wild and requiring urgent attention.
2026-07-17.action: Identify all Fortinet FortiSandbox appliances in your environment and apply the latest security patches provided by Fortinet without delay. Isolate affected systems if patching cannot be done immediately to contain potential compromise.
2026-07-17.affected_versions: Fortinet FortiSandbox threat detection platform (specific vulnerable versions require immediate vendor advisory check)
2026-07-17.how_to_determine_impact: Check the firmware version of all FortiSandbox instances against Fortinet's official security advisories. Look for any reported Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) within the FortiSandbox logs or network traffic associated with these devices.
2026-07-17.tanium_query_hint: Get Network Interfaces and Network Connections from all machines with 'FortiSandbox' in Installed Applications or by network device identification. Look for suspicious outbound connections or unusual processes on FortiSandbox appliances.
2026-07-17.rapid7_query_hint: Search for Fortinet vulnerabilities in InsightVM/Nexpose, filtering for FortiSandbox products. Monitor InsightIDR for suspicious activity originating from FortiSandbox devices or network segments where they reside, especially unusual administrative access.
2026-07-17.cvss_score: 9.0 Critical (Estimate based on CISA warning for actively exploited flaws in a security appliance)

2026-07-17.title: Claude Chrome extension flaw lets malicious extensions trigger AI actions
2026-07-17.tldr: A vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude for Chrome browser extension could allow other malicious browser extensions to simulate user clicks and trigger predefined AI actions. This could lead to unauthorized abuse of Claude's access to integrated services like Gmail, Google Docs, and Salesforce.
2026-07-17.action: Users should audit their installed Chrome extensions, removing any suspicious or unnecessary ones. Anthropic users should monitor for official updates to the Claude extension and disable it if no patch is immediately available.
2026-07-17.affected_versions: Anthropic's Claude for Chrome browser extension (specific vulnerable versions require vendor advisory check)
2026-07-17.how_to_determine_impact: Inspect installed Chrome extensions on user workstations. Identify systems with the Claude extension. Review browser activity logs and audit logs of connected services (Gmail, Google Docs, Calendar, Salesforce) for unexpected or unauthorized actions performed via the extension.
2026-07-17.tanium_query_hint: Get Browser Extensions from all machines; identify systems with 'Claude' extension and check its version. Monitor web activity logs for unusual API calls originating from browser processes associated with the Claude extension.
2026-07-17.rapid7_query_hint: Search for 'Claude extension' or 'browser extension vulnerabilities' in asset inventory. Monitor for unusual activity on Google and Salesforce accounts linked to users with the Claude extension via InsightIDR, looking for abnormal API usage or data access.
2026-07-17.cvss_score: 7.5 High (Estimate based on potential for unauthorized actions and data access via malicious extension interaction)

2026-07-17.title: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
2026-07-17.tldr: The GoldenEyeDog subgroup is implicated in a data breach at DigiCert, leading to the theft of code-signing certificates. This compromise allows attackers to sign malicious software with legitimate certificates, evading security controls and trust mechanisms.
2026-07-17.action: Revoke any code-signing certificates associated with DigiCert that may have been compromised. Implement strict software integrity checks and monitor for executables signed with suspicious or newly revoked certificates. Update trust policies to reflect the breach.

2026-07-17.title: Ernst & Young discloses data breach after support system hack
2026-07-17.tldr: Ernst & Young has confirmed a data breach resulting from the compromise of a third-party support ticket system used by its IT staff. Customer data may have been exposed, leading to potential privacy violations and further targeted attacks.
2026-07-17.action: Notify potentially affected customers immediately. Investigate the full scope of the breach, including what specific data was accessed or exfiltrated. Implement enhanced security measures for all third-party vendors and conduct a thorough review of incident response plans.

2026-07-17.title: Coca-Cola Suspends US Fairlife Production Due to Ransomware Attack
2026-07-17.tldr: The Coca-Cola Company has confirmed that a ransomware attack impacted its Fairlife dairy subsidiary, leading to a halt in US dairy production. This incident highlights the critical need for robust ransomware defenses and comprehensive business continuity planning.
2026-07-17.action: Isolate compromised systems, initiate data recovery from secure, offsite backups, and conduct a full forensic investigation. Strengthen cybersecurity posture with emphasis on ransomware prevention, including multi-factor authentication, privileged access management, and regular security awareness training.

2026-07-17.title: Fresh SharePoint Vulnerability Exploited Soon After Disclosure
2026-07-17.tldr: A newly disclosed vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint is already being actively exploited in the wild, underscoring the urgency for organizations to apply patches. The rapid exploitation indicates a critical flaw that threat actors are quick to leverage for unauthorized access or further compromise.
2026-07-17.action: Immediately apply the latest security patches for Microsoft SharePoint. If patching is not feasible, implement temporary mitigations such as network segmentation and restricting external access to SharePoint servers. Conduct a forensic review for signs of compromise on affected systems.
2026-07-17.affected_versions: Microsoft SharePoint. Specific vulnerable versions should be referenced from official Microsoft advisories.
2026-07-17.how_to_determine_impact: Check all deployed Microsoft SharePoint servers for the presence of the vulnerability. Verify patch levels against the official Microsoft security advisory. Look for signs of compromise on unpatched systems, such as unusual file creations, process executions, or network connections from SharePoint servers.
2026-07-17.tanium_query_hint: Get Installed Applications with Name contains 'SharePoint' and Version not in '[patched_versions_list]'. Use 'Get Network Connections from all SharePoint servers where destination IP is not in internal ranges'.
